title: AI, ML & Deep Learning
description: Intelligent systems, machine learning models, neural networks, computer vision, and generative AI.
overview: This domain focuses on building intelligent systems that learn from data. It spans supervised and unsupervised learning, deep neural networks, computer vision, and natural language processing — culminating in modern generative AI applications.

projects.name: Spam Detection
projects.desc: Build a classifier to identify spam from ham email messages.

projects.name: Performance Predictor
projects.desc: Predict student academic performance from historical data.

projects.name: Movie Recommender
projects.desc: Collaborative and content-based movie recommendation engine.

projects.name: Image Classification
projects.desc: CNN that classifies images into multiple object categories.

miniQuiz.q: Do you enjoy mathematical problem-solving and finding patterns in data?
Are you comfortable with Python and basic statistics?
Do you prefer research-oriented tasks over building final UI products?
Are you fascinated by neural networks and generative models?

steps.title: Core Programming
steps.desc: Master Python — data structures, OOP, and basic algorithms. Build foundational scripting skills.

steps.title: Applied Mathematics
steps.desc: Deep-dive into Statistics, Probability, Linear Algebra, and Calculus. These form the backbone of all ML.

steps.title: Machine Learning Algorithms
steps.desc: Learn Supervised, Unsupervised, and Reinforcement Learning. Practice with Scikit-Learn.

steps.title: Deep Learning & Neural Networks
steps.desc: Understand ANNs, CNNs, and RNNs. Gain hands-on experience using TensorFlow or PyTorch.

steps.title: Specialisation — NLP or CV
steps.desc: Specialise in text processing (Transformers, LLMs) or image processing (YOLO, ResNets).

steps.title: Deployment & MLOps
steps.desc: Wrap models into APIs using FastAPI, then deploy with Docker, AWS, or Hugging Face.

year1.title: Core Programming
year1.desc: Mastering Python logic, data structures, and advanced OOP principles for robust AI development.

year1.title: Applied Mathematics
year1.desc: Statistical modeling, linear algebra, and calculus fundamentals required for algorithm design.

year2.title: Machine Learning Algorithms
year2.desc: Deep dive into supervised and unsupervised learning using industry-standard libraries like Scikit-Learn.

year3.title: Deep Learning & Neural Networks
year3.desc: Architecting neural networks (CNNs, RNNs) and implementing them using TensorFlow and PyTorch.

year3.title: Specialisation — NLP or CV
year3.desc: Advanced specialization in Large Language Models (Transformers) or Computer Vision systems.

year4.title: Deployment & MLOps
year4.desc: Production-grade deployment using Docker, FastAPI, and specialized cloud AI platforms.

resources.title: Coursera: DeepLearning.AI
resources.desc: Comprehensive deep learning foundation by Andrew Ng.

resources.title: Kaggle
resources.desc: Datasets, competitions, and community notebooks to practice real-world data science.

resources.title: Hugging Face Course
resources.desc: State-of-the-art NLP and Transformers tutorial.

resources.title: Fast.ai
resources.desc: Practical deep learning for coders with a 'top-down' approach.

resources.title: Machine Learning Mastery
resources.desc: Step-by-step tutorials for almost every ML problem.

title: Data Science
description: Data analysis, visualization, statistics, and predictive techniques to drive better decisions.
overview: Data Science involves collecting, cleaning, and analysing large datasets to uncover actionable insights. Practitioners use statistical models, data visualisation tools, and predictive algorithms to support business strategy.

projects.name: Sales Dashboard
projects.desc: Interactive dashboard tracking revenue, trends, and KPIs.

projects.name: Student Result Analysis
projects.desc: Visualise patterns and performance distribution across a cohort.

projects.name: Customer Segmentation
projects.desc: K-means clustering to group customers by behavioural patterns.

projects.name: Trend Analysis Dashboard
projects.desc: Time-series visualisation for identifying business trends.

miniQuiz.q: Do you like finding stories hidden in numbers and charts?
Is SQL or Excel something you enjoy working with?
Would you rather analyze a problem than build a piece of hardware?
Does the idea of predicting business trends excite you?

steps.title: Programming & Databases
steps.desc: Master Python or R. Deeply understand SQL for querying large datasets.

steps.title: Data Wrangling & EDA
steps.desc: Use Pandas and NumPy to clean messy data. Find patterns and anomalies.

steps.title: Data Visualisation
steps.desc: Communicate insights visually using Tableau, Power BI, or Python libraries.

steps.title: Applied Statistics
steps.desc: Learn A/B testing, hypothesis testing, and inferential statistics.

steps.title: Predictive Modelling
steps.desc: Apply ML models for forecasting, clustering, and classification.

steps.title: Big Data Technologies
steps.desc: Scale with Apache Spark, Hadoop, and cloud data warehouses like Snowflake.

year1.title: Programming & Databases
year1.desc: Advanced SQL querying and Python-based data manipulation for analytical pipelines.

year1.title: Data Wrangling & EDA
year1.desc: Professional data cleaning techniques and exploratory analysis to find hidden patterns.

year2.title: Data Visualisation
year2.desc: Mastering the art of storytelling through numbers via Power BI, Tableau, and D3.js.

year3.title: Applied Statistics
year3.desc: Implementation of A/B testing, hypothesis testing, and inferential statistics in business scenarios.

year3.title: Predictive Modelling
year3.desc: Building forecasting systems and classification models for real-world impact.

year4.title: Big Data Technologies
year4.desc: Scaling analytics using Apache Spark, Hadoop, and cloud-based data warehouses.

resources.title: DataCamp
resources.desc: Interactive Python and SQL data science courses.

resources.title: Google Data Analytics
resources.desc: Industry-standard professional certification for analysts.

resources.title: Towards Data Science
resources.desc: Leading publication for data science techniques and trends.

resources.title: StatQuest
resources.desc: Breakdown of complex statistical concepts into simple visuals.

title: Web, App & Game Development
description: Websites, mobile applications, full-stack software systems, and interactive game experiences.
overview: This domain spans the full stack of digital product development — from crafting pixel-perfect interfaces with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, to building robust backend APIs, mobile applications, and interactive video games.

projects.name: Portfolio Website
projects.desc: A personal site showcasing projects, skills, and contact info.

projects.name: To-Do Application
projects.desc: Full-stack CRUD app with persistent storage and user auth.

projects.name: Chat Application
projects.desc: Real-time messaging app using WebSockets.

projects.name: Mobile Calculator
projects.desc: Cross-platform calculator built with Flutter.

insights.futureScope: Web3, XR interfaces, and immersive game worlds

miniQuiz.q: Do you love seeing your code turn into something visual immediately?
Are you interested in how mobile apps and websites are built?
Do you enjoy the challenge of making things look good AND work well?
Does the idea of building your own video game sound fun?

steps.title: Web Foundations
steps.desc: Master HTML, CSS and core JavaScript (DOM, events, ES6+).

steps.title: Frontend Frameworks
steps.desc: Learn React.js for component-driven UI and state management.

steps.title: Backend & Databases
steps.desc: Build REST APIs using Node.js/Express. Learn MySQL and MongoDB.

steps.title: Full Stack Integration
steps.desc: Connect frontend and backend, implement auth, and deploy apps.

steps.title: Mobile Development
steps.desc: Learn React Native or Flutter for iOS and Android publication.

steps.title: Game Development
steps.desc: Create games using Unity (C#) or web-based game libraries.

year1.title: Web Foundations
year1.desc: Deep dive into semantic HTML, modern CSS (Flex/Grid), and advanced ES6+ JavaScript.

year1.title: Frontend Frameworks
year1.desc: Component architecture, state management, and high-performance rendering in React.js.

year2.title: Backend & Databases
year2.desc: Building secure, scalable RESTful and GraphQL APIs using Node.js and specialized DBs.

year3.title: Full Stack Integration
year3.desc: End-to-end product development including authentication, Real-time sync, and deployment.

year3.title: Mobile Development
year3.desc: Crafting native-like experiences for iOS and Android using Flutter or React Native.

year4.title: Game Development
year4.desc: Building interactive 2D/3D web-based and standalone game engines.

resources.title: MDN Web Docs
resources.desc: The ultimate resource for web technologies and documentation.

resources.title: FreeCodeCamp
resources.desc: Global community for learning full stack development hands-on.

resources.title: frontendmasters
resources.desc: In-depth courses from world-class industry engineers.

resources.title: roadmap.sh
resources.desc: Visual career pathways for every modern web specialisation.

title: Cyber Security & Blockchain
description: System security, ethical hacking, cryptography, and decentralized application development.
overview: This domain covers protecting computing systems, networks, and data from attacks. It also spans the development of decentralised systems using blockchain — from smart contracts to on-chain apps.

projects.name: Password strength checker
projects.desc: Evaluate password entropy and resistance to attack vectors.

projects.name: Vulnerability Scanner
projects.desc: Detect open ports and outdated services on a target host.

projects.name: Phishing Detector
projects.desc: ML-based classifier that identifies malicious links.

projects.name: Simple Smart Contract
projects.desc: Deploy a basic ERC-20 token on a test blockchain network.

insights.futureScope: AI-driven threat detection and Zero Trust

miniQuiz.q: Do you think about how to break systems to make them stronger?
Are you interested in cryptography and private data?
Do you enjoy the 'cat and mouse' game of finding exploits?
Does the idea of decentralized web (Blockchain) intrigue you?

steps.title: IT & Networking Foundations
steps.desc: Master TCP/IP, DNS, OSI model, and Linux internals.

steps.title: Security Fundamentals
steps.desc: Learn CIA triad, auth mechanisms, and CompTIA Security+ syllabus.

steps.title: Offensive Security
steps.desc: Study OWASP Top 10 and practice on TryHackMe or HackTheBox.

steps.title: Defensive Security
steps.desc: Understand SIEM (Splunk), digital forensics, and incident response.

steps.title: Malware Analysis
steps.desc: Learn assembly basics and reverse-engineer binaries with Ghidra.

steps.title: Cloud Security & Advanced
steps.desc: Branch into Cloud audits, DevSecOps, and OSCP certification.

year1.title: IT & Networking Foundations
year1.desc: Comprehensive understanding of the OSI model, TCP/IP, and Linux kernel internals.

year1.title: Security Fundamentals
year1.desc: Introduction to cryptography, CIA triad, and baseline security protocols.

year2.title: Offensive Security
year2.desc: Techniques for ethical hacking, penetration testing, and vulnerability research.

year3.title: Defensive Security
year3.desc: Incident response, SOC operations, and building resilient security architectures.

year3.title: Malware Analysis
year3.desc: Reverse engineering binaries and analyzing malicious software behavior.

year4.title: Cloud Security & Advanced
year4.desc: Securing distributed systems and deep dive into Blockchain infrastructure.

resources.title: TryHackMe
resources.desc: Gamified labs for learning real-world cybersecurity.

resources.title: Krebs on Security
resources.desc: In-depth investigative journalism on global cybercrime.

resources.title: OWASP Hot 10
resources.desc: Standard awareness document for web application security.

resources.title: CryptoZombies
resources.desc: Interactive tutorial for building blockchain smart contracts.

title: Cloud Computing
description: Scalable virtualization, serverless architecture, containerization, and cloud deployment strategies.
overview: Cloud Computing delivers on-demand computing services over the internet. Practitioners design and manage scalable infrastructure using virtualisation, containers, and pipelines across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

projects.name: Static Website (AWS)
projects.desc: Host a highly available site using S3 and CloudFront.

projects.name: Serverless API
projects.desc: REST API using AWS Lambda and API Gateway.

projects.name: Dockerised Web App
projects.desc: Containerise and deploy using Docker and K8s.

projects.name: Terraform Infrastructure
projects.desc: Automate cloud resource provisioning with scripts.

miniQuiz.q: Are you interested in how websites handle millions of users?
Do you enjoy automating tasks with scripts?
Does managing virtual servers and containers sound cool?
Are you more interested in infrastructure than frontend pixels?

steps.title: Linux & Scripting
steps.desc: Master CLI, file systems, and automation in Bash/Python.

steps.title: Networking Essentials
steps.desc: Understand DNS, firewalls, subnetting, VPNs, and load balancers.

steps.title: Core Cloud Services
steps.desc: Learn EC2, S3, RDS, and IAM on AWS, Azure, or GCP.

steps.title: Containers & K8s
steps.desc: Package apps with Docker and orchestrate with Kubernetes.

steps.title: IaC & CI/CD
steps.desc: Provision with Terraform and automate with GitHub Actions.

steps.title: SRE & Cloud Security
steps.desc: Apply SRE principles and implement secure IAM policies.

year1.title: Linux & Scripting
year1.desc: CLI mastery and automation scripts in Bash and Python for infrastructure orchestration.

year1.title: Networking Essentials
year1.desc: Configuring VPCs, subnets, firewalls, and load balancers in hybrid environments.

year2.title: Core Cloud Services
year2.desc: Hands-on implementation of compute, storage, and database services on AWS, Azure, and GCP.

year3.title: Containers & K8s
year3.desc: Package-level virtualization with Docker and massive-scale orchestration with Kubernetes.

year3.title: IaC & CI/CD
year3.desc: Infrastructure as Code using Terraform and automated delivery pipelines with GitHub Actions.

year4.title: SRE & Cloud Security
year4.desc: Building high-availability systems with robust IAM policies and observability.

title: IoT & Embedded Systems
description: Smart devices, microcontrollers, sensors, and the integration of hardware with software.
overview: This domain focuses on programming microcontrollers to interact with the physical world through sensors and wireless communication — forming the foundation of smart homes and industry.

projects.name: Smart Light Control
projects.desc: Remotely control lights via a mobile app or voice.

projects.name: Temperature Monitor
projects.desc: Real-time tracking with data logging.

projects.name: Smart Irrigation
projects.desc: Automate plant watering using moisture sensors.

projects.name: IoT Attendance
projects.desc: RFID-based tracker logging to cloud database.

miniQuiz.q: Do you enjoy working with microcontrollers and sensors?
Are you interested in building smart devices (homes, wearables)?
Does writing low-level C code for hardware excite you?
Do you prefer physical building over pure software simulation?

steps.desc: Learn Ohm's law, circuits, digital logic, and breadboarding.

steps.title: Embedded C/C++
steps.desc: Understand pointers, interrupts, and register manipulation.

steps.title: Arduino & ESP32

steps.desc: Run Linux, write Python hardware scripts, and build smart apps.

steps.title: Cloud & Platforms
steps.desc: Connect to Firebase or AWS IoT and build monitoring dashboards.

title: Robotics & Automation
description: Robot design, automation systems, machine control, and intelligent physical systems.
overview: Robotics focuses on designing autonomous systems that can sense, plan, and act in physical environments — from wheeled robots to industrial automation arms.

projects.name: Line-Following Robot
projects.desc: IR sensor-based robot that autonomously follows a path.

projects.name: Obstacle-Avoiding Robot
projects.desc: Ultrasonic sensor-based bot that navigates around obstacles.

projects.name: Robotic Arm Prototype
projects.desc: Servo-controlled arm capable of picking and placing objects.

projects.name: Home Automation
projects.desc: Control home appliances through voice or mobile.

Do you enjoy combining mechanical parts with software logic?
Is the idea of building a robotic arm or self-driving car exciting?

title: VLSI & Computer Architecture
description: Chip design, circuit layout, processor architecture, and the fundamentals of hardware.
overview: This domain covers the design and verification of digital circuits and CPUs — from logic gates to complete pipelines using HDL and FPGA platforms.

projects.name: ALU Design
projects.desc: Arithmetic Logic Unit in Verilog performing basic operations.

projects.name: Traffic Light Controller
projects.desc: Sequential circuit modelling a traffic signal.

projects.name: Simple CPU Simulation
projects.desc: Fetch-decode-execute cycle implemented in HDL.

projects.name: Digital Counter
projects.desc: 4-bit synchronous counter with enable controls.

insights.futureScope: AI chips and next-gen edge processors

miniQuiz.q: Do you enjoy logic puzzles and digital circuit design?
Are you interested in how CPUs and microchips work inside?
Does writing hardware description languages (Verilog) sound fun?
Do you prefer low-level hardware design over high-level app building?

steps.title: Digital Logic
steps.desc: Master number systems, Boolean algebra, and K-maps.

steps.title: Circuit Design

steps.title: Physical Design
